breast.definition: The part of a woman's body that produces milk to feed a baby.
breast.example: The baby was feeding from the breast.

crawl.definition: To move forward on hands and knees, or very slowly.
crawl.example: The baby learned to crawl before walking.

hurry.definition: To do something quickly or to rush.
hurry.example: There is no need to hurry; we have plenty of time.

labour.definition: The process of giving birth, when the body pushes the baby out.
labour.example: She went into labour early in the morning.

breastfeed.definition: To feed a baby with milk from the mother's breast.
breastfeed.example: Many doctors recommend that mothers breastfeed their babies.

wean.definition: To gradually stop feeding a baby breast milk and start giving other food.
wean.example: She started to wean her baby at six months.

independent.definition: Able to do things by yourself without help from others.
independent.example: He became more independent after moving out.

property.definition: A building or land that someone owns.
property.example: They saved money to buy their first property.

ladder.definition: A series of steps to climb up; 'property ladder' means starting to own a home.
ladder.example: Getting on the property ladder is hard for young people.

retirement.definition: The time when you stop working, usually when you are old.
retirement.example: She is saving money for her retirement.

observe.example: Scientists observe animals in their natural environment.

rush.definition: A state of hurrying; doing things too quickly.
rush.example: Do not be in a rush; take your time.

present.example: Try to live more in the present and worry less about the future.

incredible.example: The view from the mountain was incredible.

seeking.definition: Looking for something; trying to find.
seeking.example: The baby has a natural seeking behavior for food.

mechanism.definition: A natural process or system that does a particular thing.
mechanism.example: Crying is a mechanism babies use to get attention.

institute.definition: An organization for research or education.
institute.example: She works at a science research institute.

newborn.definition: A baby that was just recently born.
newborn.example: The newborn slept most of the day.

abdomen.definition: The front part of the body between the chest and the legs; the belly area.
abdomen.example: The doctor examined her abdomen carefully.

propel.definition: To push or move something forward.
propel.example: The baby used their legs to propel themselves forward.

alternating.definition: Switching back and forth between two things.
alternating.example: The pattern had alternating red and blue stripes.

bursts.definition: Short periods of sudden strong activity.
bursts.example: The baby moved in short bursts of energy.

gripping.example: A newborn has a strong gripping reflex in their hands.

reflex.definition: An automatic body movement that you do not control.
reflex.example: Blinking is a natural reflex to protect the eyes.

nipple.definition: The small raised part on the breast where milk comes out.
nipple.example: The baby found the nipple and started to feed.

stimulate.definition: To cause a reaction or make something start working.
stimulate.example: The touch helped stimulate milk production.

sucking.definition: Drawing something into the mouth by creating a vacuum.
sucking.example: Sucking is one of a baby's first natural actions.

rooting.definition: A baby's reflex of turning their head to search for the breast.
rooting.example: The rooting reflex helps newborns find food.

infant.example: The infant was only three days old.

spontaneously.definition: Happening naturally without being planned or forced.
spontaneously.example: The baby spontaneously reached for the toy.

suckle.definition: To drink milk from a mother's breast.
suckle.example: The lamb began to suckle from its mother.

subsequent.definition: Coming after something else in time.
subsequent.example: The first study and all subsequent studies showed the same result.

apparently.definition: Based on what seems to be true; it appears that.
apparently.example: Apparently, the store closes early on Sundays.

amniotic.definition: Related to the fluid that surrounds a baby inside the mother's body.
amniotic.example: The amniotic fluid protects the baby during pregnancy.

instinctive.definition: Done naturally without thinking; based on instinct.
instinctive.example: A mother's love for her baby is instinctive.

drive.definition: A strong natural need or desire to do something.
drive.example: Hunger is a basic drive that makes us eat.

norm.example: Crying is the norm for newborn babies.

mammals.definition: Animals that feed their babies with milk from the mother's body.
mammals.example: Dogs, cats, and humans are all mammals.

variety.definition: A number of different kinds or types.
variety.example: The store sells a variety of fruits.

reflexes.definition: Automatic body responses that happen without thinking.
reflexes.example: Babies are born with several important reflexes.

facilitating.definition: Making something easier or helping it happen.
facilitating.example: The teacher is facilitating the group discussion.

survival.example: Food and water are necessary for survival.

obvious.definition: Easy to see or understand; clear.
obvious.example: It was obvious that she was tired.

cot.definition: A small bed with high sides for a baby.
cot.example: The baby was sleeping in the cot.

whereas.definition: Used to compare two different things or situations.
whereas.example: She likes coffee, whereas her sister prefers tea.

control.definition: A group in a study that does not receive the treatment, used for comparison.
control.example: The control group did not receive the new medicine.

keen.definition: Very eager or wanting to do something strongly.
keen.example: We were too keen to help and ended up doing too much.

interfere.definition: To get in the way of something; to prevent a natural process.
interfere.example: Please do not interfere with the experiment.

pain-relieving.example: The doctor gave her pain-relieving medicine.

caesarean.definition: A surgery to deliver a baby through a cut in the mother's belly.
caesarean.example: She had a caesarean because the baby was in a difficult position.

denied.definition: Not allowed to have something; prevented from getting something.
denied.example: Many children are denied a proper education.

self-propelling.definition: Moving by one's own power without outside help.
self-propelling.example: The baby made a self-propelling movement toward the breast.

balanced.definition: Stable; having different parts in the right amounts.
balanced.example: She is a well-balanced person who handles stress calmly.

functioning.definition: Working or operating in a proper way.
functioning.example: He grew up to be a fully functioning adult.

bonds.example: The bonds between the siblings were very strong.

cues.definition: Signs or signals that tell you what to do or what is happening.
cues.example: Parents learn to read their baby's cues.

rhythm.definition: A regular pattern of actions or events.
rhythm.example: They fell into a natural rhythm of feeding and sleeping.

merely.example: I was merely trying to help.

gazing.definition: Looking at something steadily for a long time.
gazing.example: The baby was gazing at her mother's face.

respect.definition: Treating someone as important and worthy of attention.
respect.example: We should treat children with respect.

agency.definition: The power to act and make choices for yourself.
agency.example: Even babies have a sense of agency in the world.
